⚠ #general @Ilmen 2025-11-09 #w5_9xw99u $en +2
▯ is a general chat channel hosted in Toadua.
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Jadı
  kazoo (2025-11-09): lol
  kazoo (2025-11-09): reposting: i dont have access to discord rn but i wanna ask smth rq, Assuming kaı and fa as example predicates, is kaı sá sá fâ sá always equivalent to fa wä kaı sá sá sá
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Damned “remove” button
  kazoo (2025-11-09): why not bridge the matrix server
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): I think the ^ tone semantics assume ë instead of ꝡä
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): But I think it's fine to allow for ꝡä
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Probably depending on the type of the verb on which ^ is put
  kazoo (2025-11-09): makes sense
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): The ꝡä semantics are needed for using stuff like ⟪guo⟫ and ⟪le⟫ as adverbs
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): And I even suspect this also applies to stuff like ⟪ca⟫ and ⟪rao⟫
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Alternatively, it could be that the meaning of “event” is more abstract that what I supposed thus far, in such a way that the ë-ꝡä distinction wouldn't really be meaningful…
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Besides Matrix, we could also use GitHub Discussion (i.e. GitHub forums)
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Any repository can have a GitHub Discussion attached to it
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): I don't know whether the GitHub notification bot that relays Issues message would also relay stuff from Discussions
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Oh there's also the Conlangs From Space forum
  kazoo (2025-11-09): iirc wä is a subset of ë where the speaker also claims the event happened. am i recalling correctly?
  evie (2025-11-09): no
  kazoo (2025-11-09): also i wonder if shu includes the tone or does it just quote the root
  evie (2025-11-09): wa is 'the (fact/proposition/statement) that'
  evie (2025-11-09): shu does not steal tone. shu rao, shu jí, shu wä, shu nîe
  evie (2025-11-09): shú whatever / ké shu whatever turns it into a noun
  kazoo (2025-11-09): i know but i mean is shú rao the same as shú ráo
  evie (2025-11-09): no
  kazoo (2025-11-09): then what is the fundamental difference between an event of... and a fact of...
  kazoo (2025-11-09): (unrelated to shu)
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): A proposition is abstract, it contains only the information that is given in it and nothing more; a proposition can entail any less detailed version of it (“a tall man wears a blue hat” → “a man wears a hat”), and any more detailed proposition similarly entails the first one, forming a hierarchy of level of detailedness / amount of information.
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): The way I understand events is that they are individual entities, presumably spatiotemporal entities but I think it's possible to extend them to non-spationtempral worlds (so that “an event of 1 = √1” would make sense)
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): It is possible to conceive individuals and propositions as belonging to a single supertype, in such a way that an individual is an infinitely or maximally detailed proposition, so that, unlike standard proposition, an event cannot entail or be instantiated by a “more detailed event”
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): So that it would be like a terminal node in the hierarchy of abstractness/detailedness
  kazoo (2025-11-09): 
  kazoo (2025-11-09): 
  kazoo (2025-11-09): i keep hitting the enter button
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): As a side note, ⟪ꝡä⟫ and ⟪báq ë⟫ could possibly be equivalent in effect
  kazoo (2025-11-09): :thinking emoji:
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): But I'm not sure, the semantics of ⟪báq⟫ seem to have changed in Kúna, and I've not yet studied new-báq…
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Anyway, people say that tone ^ is associated with events, but this might clash with my theory of “events are indiviuals reifying propositions”
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): I've started to doubt that verbs like ⟦ca⟧ deal with individual events instead of propositions
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): When you say ⟪pushing this button causes the door to open⟫, you're not thinking of a specific fully detailed and concrete event of pushing the button
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): If you did, we wouldn't know whether the real cause would be unspoken details about the event
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): But with an adverb like ⟪de⟫, you do want to talk about unspoken details
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): ⟪he danced funnily⟫ means that some unspoken details of a concrete event instantiating ⟪he dances⟫ are funny
  evie (2025-11-09): yea i think shê works better there. shê wá someone pushes the button nä the door opens
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): it's not just the abstract idea that he dances that is funny
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Maybe we could also say that ⟪dê⟫ is actually not good and that we should use ⟪châ sá de⟫
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Or we could say that ⟪de⟫'s type require an individual, concrete reading, unlike e.g. ⟪ca⟫
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): *requires
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): It's not just ⟪ca⟫, imagine also ⟪boq⟫
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): I suspect more predicates that we might expect actually expect abstract, non-concrete events (i.e. propositions)
  kazoo (2025-11-09): i wonder what the wa - wä pair for ë would even be
  evie (2025-11-09): e is hardly ever used as the main verb of a sentence
  kazoo (2025-11-09): or if it would even make sense
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Well, speech acts are typically about ꝡä or embedded questions, not about concrete event arguments
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Oh I tend to forget ⟪é⟫ isn't a (regular) complementizer like ꝡa
  kazoo (2025-11-09): unrelatedly, ive been working on a new language whose grammar could be considered improved toaq gamma with a hint of delta proposals
  kazoo (2025-11-09): a lot of my langs lately have been coincidentally similar to toaq so i've decided to embrace the toaqness now
  kazoo (2025-11-09): it takes some surface-level inspiration with other sinitic languages with its phonology
  kazoo (2025-11-09): it only has ë and lä clauses as complementizers and the other clause "type"s are constructed by just attaching it to a noun as a plain ol ~adjective
  kazoo (2025-11-09): so that it can be more flexible
  kazoo (2025-11-09): (c.f. 300 different NU particles of lojban)
  kazoo (2025-11-09): it tries to bring the "everything is a predicate" even further
  kazoo (2025-11-09): compared to toaq im planning to make it basically like loglan vs lojban except its a coexisting fun alternative rather than an improvement
  kazoo (2025-11-09): i think toaq as it is is already cool, i just like to make stuff that would keep the level of coolness
  kazoo (2025-11-09): it also primarily switches to monadic second-(/higher?-)order logic which is expressively the same as plural logic but uses sets which is more intuitive to me
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Nothing wrong with making new Toaq-inspired languages :')
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): You can have modified sets… You probably don't want empty sets in the way, do you?
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): On the other hand, nested sets may be useful
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Some forms of Plural Logic allows for nested plurals, but with important constrants
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): E.g. you can have the equivalent of {{A, B}, {C, D}}, but not {{A, B}, C, D}
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): And the former is not of the same type as {A, B}
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): (first-order plural vs second-order plural, basically)
  kazoo (2025-11-09): i see
  kazoo (2025-11-09): i also briefly considered abolishing the universal quantifier by using a special object incorporation construction, basically resulting as "there is a set that has an instance of all humans; which satisfies aliveness"
  kazoo (2025-11-09): but i do not know if this would work for all instances of universal quantification
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): With empty sets allowed, and saying that each member of the set individually has the property, then it works for a singular universal quantifier
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Eberban doesn't have quantifiers and use predicates instead
  kazoo (2025-11-09): yeah im just gonna make it sá by default and make the rest with predicates
  kazoo (2025-11-09): i decided that because i randomly remembered once that the wiki says to append shı to the argument to mimic singularity and i thought "why is this not made for every determiner??"
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): There used to be ⟪túshı⟫ at one point in the past
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): But it was decided that it was more frequently used than plural universal, so ⟪túshı⟫'s meaning was transposed over ⟪tú⟫
  kazoo (2025-11-09): thats a little bit funny lol, the newer concept being swapped with the normal one
  kazoo (2025-11-09): tho i meant that every determiner should instead be a predicate
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Have you looked into my Yaıgawe? All quantifiers are derived from verbs with a “quantifierizer” suffix.
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Nahaıwa can do that too, but it has morphological shorthand for some common determiners
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): As for what is the unmarked default quantifier for them… Originally my idea was that the default would be lowest-scope existential quantification, but now I'm considering the possibility to have the default be a báq-like intension (i.e. not necessarily referring to a concrete individual)
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): So that ⟪beautiful-V dog-NOM⟫ would mean ⟪dogness is beautiful⟫ rather than ⟪there exist a beautiful dog⟫
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Hmm maybe this should depend on modality too
  Ilmen (2025-11-09): Oh, don't forget the anaphoric determiner, Toaq's raising tone, which can't quite be rendered as a predicate
  kazoo (2025-11-09): hm, maybe i could handle it by just offering hu/ke options or using back-referring pronouns
  kazoo (2025-11-09): if i (la'anai) manage to not use up most of the tones i have added to the phonology i could use one for that
  kazoo (2025-11-09): currently have like 6 or 7 of them
  kazoo (2025-11-09): i have found use for 5 of them currently
